problemTitle: Remove Nth Node From End of List

sources.code:
class Solution:
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: ListNode, n: int) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        left = dummy
        right = head

        while n > 0:
            right = right.next
            n -= 1

        while right:
            left = left.next
            right = right.next

        # delete
        left.next = left.next.next
        return dummy.next
sources.originalCode:
class Solution:
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: ListNode, n: int) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        left = dummy
        right = head

        while n > 0:
            right = right.next
            n -= 1

        while right:
            left = left.next
            right = right.next

        # delete
        left.next = left.next.next
        return dummy.next
sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Maintain a fixed distance so later structure becomes available exactly when you need it.
class Solution:
    # [Reader note] State setup
    # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Sentinel use, offset pointers, and deletion around boundary cases such as removing the head.
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: ListNode, n: int) -> ListNode:
        dummy = ListNode(0, head)
        left = dummy
        right = head

        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        while n > 0:
            right = right.next
            n -= 1

        while right:
            left = left.next
            right = right.next

        # delete
        left.next = left.next.next
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return dummy.next

sources.localPath: doocs_leetcode/solution/0000-0099/0019.Remove Nth Node From End of List/Solution.py
sources.relativePath: solution/0000-0099/0019.Remove Nth Node From End of List/Solution.py
sources.code:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: Optional[ListNode], n: int) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(next=head)
        fast = slow = dummy
        for _ in range(n):
            fast = fast.next
        while fast.next:
            slow, fast = slow.next, fast.next
        slow.next = slow.next.next
        return dummy.next
sources.originalCode:
# Definition for singly-linked list.
# class ListNode:
#     def __init__(self, val=0, next=None):
#         self.val = val
#         self.next = next
class Solution:
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: Optional[ListNode], n: int) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(next=head)
        fast = slow = dummy
        for _ in range(n):
            fast = fast.next
        while fast.next:
            slow, fast = slow.next, fast.next
        slow.next = slow.next.next
        return dummy.next

class Solution:
    def removeNthFromEnd(self, head: Optional[ListNode], n: int) -> Optional[ListNode]:
        dummy = ListNode(next=head)
        fast = slow = dummy
        # [Reader note] Main pass
        # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
        for _ in range(n):
            fast = fast.next
        while fast.next:
            slow, fast = slow.next, fast.next
        slow.next = slow.next.next
        # [Reader note] Return
        # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
        return dummy.next

sources.reviewedCode:
# [Reader note] Entry point
# Start from the core routine and keep this mental model in view: Maintain a fixed distance so later structure becomes available exactly when you need it.
def removeNthFromEnd(self, head, n):
    # [Reader note] State setup
    # This setup stage creates the state needed for the invariant or partial result: Sentinel use, offset pointers, and deletion around boundary cases such as removing the head.
    slow = fast = head
    # [Reader note] Main pass
    # This is the main sweep where the implementation earns its efficiency instead of restarting work from scratch.
    for i in range(n):
        fast = fast.next
    # [Reader note] Decision point
    # Pay attention here: The core idea is stable, but head removal is where many otherwise correct drafts break.
    if not fast: return head.next
    while fast.next:
        fast = fast.next
        slow = slow.next
    slow.next = slow.next.next
    # [Reader note] Return
    # The final return exposes the result after the traversal and state updates have settled.
    return head
